title: Ethical Consumerism
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: The modern marketplace is seeing an (0) unprecedented shift toward ethical consumption. Many (17) now scrutinize the supply chains of global corporations. This heightened (18) has forced brands to (19) their production methods. || While some critics dismiss this as mere PR, others argue it represents a (20) of corporate responsibility. The (21) of sustainable goods is no longer a niche market. However, the (22) of 'greenwashing'—where companies make (23) environmental claims—remains a concern. To (24) the impact of their purchases, shoppers are increasingly turning to independent certification labels.

questions.explanation: Noun with internal change and suffix -ity.
Noun with double consonant and suffix -ence.
